title: Research
text: My research sits at the intersection of graph representation learning and financial applications — specifically how temporal structure in transaction networks can improve fraud and risk detection.

title: Research
text:
Conference publications

Kernelized Edge Attention: Addressing Semantic Attention Blurring in Temporal GNNs
Govind Waghmare, Srini Rohan Gujulla Leel, Nikhil Tumbde, Sumedh B G, Sonia Gupta, Srikanta Bedathur
AAAI 2026 · Philadelphia, PA · February 2026
Temporal GNNs suffer from semantic attention blurring — relevant edge signals get diluted as graph neighborhoods grow over time. We propose Kernelized Edge Attention (KEA) as a mechanism to preserve edge-level semantics across temporal message passing. [Work presented at AAAI 2026.]



Learning Representations in Temporal Bipartite Financial Graphs
Pritam Kumar Nath, Govind Waghmare, Nikhil Tumbde, Nitish Kumar, Siddhartha Asthana
ICAIF 2023 · DOI: 10.1145/3604237.3626911 · Patent filed
Representation learning on temporal bipartite graphs (cardholders ↔︎ merchants) for financial fraud and risk tasks. Proposed an enhanced Temporal GNN architecture that outperformed state-of-the-art baselines on 3 out of 4 benchmark datasets for link prediction and node classification.

title: Research
text:
Patents

Methods and systems for determining risk associated with tokenization of payment cards
Patent filed — Mastercard
A risk mitigation system to detect and prevent fraudulent token provisioning at scale, enabling millisecond-latency real-time scoring across billions of annual transactions.



Methods and systems for managing default risk associated with transit transactions
Patent filed — Mastercard
A product to manage user default risk on open-loop transit networks, addressing the unique challenge of post-trip payment settlement in high-velocity transit environments.

title: Projects
text: Personal projects outside of work — focused on building ML fundamentals from scratch and applied NLP/retrieval.

title: Projects
text:
Nanograd
GitHub →
A lightweight reverse-mode automatic differentiation engine built on NumPy, with full tensor and broadcasting support. Extended Andrej Karpathy’s micrograd (scalar-level) to operate at the tensor level, enabling modular neural network construction and full tensor-level backpropagation.
Built as a ground-up exercise in understanding what frameworks like PyTorch do under the hood — forward passes, computation graphs, and the backward pass across arbitrary tensor operations.
Stack: Python · NumPy · pure autodiff

title: Projects
section: Kaggle — EEDI Mining Misconceptions in Mathematics
text:
Kaggle — EEDI Mining Misconceptions in Mathematics
Bronze Medal — Top 7%, Rank 90/1,449 teams
The task: given a student’s incorrect answer to a math problem, identify the underlying misconception from a fixed taxonomy. The challenge is that misconception labels are sparse and the retrieval space is large.
Built a two-stage pipeline:

Semantic retrieval — fine-tuned an embedding model to map (question, incorrect answer) pairs into a shared space with misconception descriptions, enabling approximate nearest-neighbor retrieval.
LLM reranker — a cross-encoder reranker refines the top-k candidates. Fine-tuned using LoRA with quantization for compute efficiency.

Synthetic training data was generated and quality-filtered using an LLM-as-judge framework to supplement the limited labeled set.
Stack: PyTorch · Transformers · LoRA · FAISS · vLLM

title: Home
text:
Senior Data Scientist · AI/ML
I build ML systems at the intersection of fraud detection, graph neural networks, and agentic AI. Currently at Mastercard’s AI Centre of Excellence, designing multi-agent pipelines for cloud operations. My work spans production fraud scoring, sequential behavioral modeling, and research on temporal graph learning — with two conference publications and three patents filed.

End-to-end fraud and risk models (XGBoost, GRU) for token provisioning and card-level behavioral embeddings. Revenue impact of $12M+, 18% TFPR reduction, two patents filed.

Oct 2022 – Apr 2023   Data Science Intern — Mastercard, AI Garage
Research on temporal GNN architectures for financial graph datasets. Work published at ICAIF’23.
